jokes.text: This could be my big chance to grovel!
jokes.setup: Learning they're delivering to a real king
jokes.punchline: Bender's excited about groveling rather than meeting royalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - he's more excited about debasing himself than the honor of meeting royalty

jokes.text: Ooh. Swanky dump.
jokes.setup: Arriving at an impressive royal palace
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling it a 'dump' despite being swanky
jokes.explanation: Classic oxymoron delivered with Bender's characteristic crude charm

jokes.text: Now, this guy knows how to grovel.
jokes.setup: Fry properly addresses the King with deference
jokes.punchline: King appreciates the groveling, validating Bender's earlier excitement
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that validates Bender's unusual priorities

jokes.text: Not even the finest macaroni artist could capture your beauty. Although this one did.
jokes.setup: Dramatic declaration about his wife's indescribable beauty
jokes.punchline: Reveals the portrait was made by a 'macaroni artist' contradicting his point
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with absurd specificity - 'macaroni artist' is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Sorry. It's my early-onset sciatica.
jokes.setup: Fry struggling to hang the portrait properly
jokes.punchline: Young person claiming early-onset sciatica as excuse
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdism - young person with old person ailment is a familiar trope but decently executed

jokes.text: Wow. This absolute stud sure made me look like a wimp, Leela?
jokes.setup: Prince helps with the portrait while Fry struggles
jokes.punchline: Fry awkwardly seeking validation from Leela about his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry insecurity - seeking reassurance while simultaneously admitting his inadequacy

jokes.text: No, no. It's nothing like that. Let N be the number of punches.
jokes.setup: King confused about simple loyalty punch card concept
jokes.punchline: Professor launches into unnecessary mathematical explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor over-complication of simple concepts with mathematical variables

jokes.text: I was falling in love with the Prince of Space. We're getting married!
jokes.setup: Leela missing for 30 minutes
jokes.punchline: Instant engagement after brief absence
jokes.explanation: Sudden romantic escalation is absurd but slightly predictable for sitcom plotting

jokes.text: I'll always remember the times we had that I haven't already forgotten.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to be sentimental about her relationship with Fry
jokes.punchline: Admits she's already forgotten most of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed line that sounds romantic but is actually devastating. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: And I can't afford to live without you!
jokes.setup: Fry declaring his love for Leela
jokes.punchline: Financial rather than emotional reason for needing her
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'afford' that reveals Fry's practical dependence while trying to be romantic

jokes.text: Good news, Windos!
jokes.setup: Professor's signature 'Good news everyone' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Addressing toys instead of the crew
jokes.explanation: Familiar running gag with slight twist - addressing products instead of people

jokes.text: Dear God, no! Pretend you didn't hear that.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg examining Fry with stethoscope
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to take back his alarmed diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg incompetence - letting slip bad news then immediately trying to cover

jokes.text: Of course, I'm sure. Fry's mainspring is nearly wound down.
jokes.setup: Question about Zoidberg's medical certainty given his usual incompetence
jokes.punchline: Confidently explains humans have mainsprings like wind-up toys
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd medical diagnosis that sets up the episode's central conceit

jokes.text: For a thousand years, he was slowly pacing and winding down.
jokes.setup: Explaining Fry's condition from being frozen
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'pacing' (movement) with 'winding down' (mechanical deterioration)
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay continuing the wind-up toy metaphor, though not exceptional

jokes.text: It was a one-way ticket to Oldsville! Last exit before Deathburg!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining Fry's aging condition
jokes.punchline: Creative place names for aging and death as travel destinations
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor with good escalation - 'Deathburg' particularly clever

jokes.text: I eated. I prayed. I loved-ed.
jokes.setup: Fry reflecting on his full life before dying
jokes.punchline: Mangled reference to 'Eat Pray Love' with wrong verb tenses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry characterization - trying to be profound but getting grammar wrong

jokes.text: Can religion calm the terror of mortality? That's our home run swing!
jokes.setup: Asked if religion can help with fear of death
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a sport with baseball metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of robot preacher treating religion like a game/sport

jokes.text: A-And we can do loop-de-loops?
jokes.setup: Hearing about flying around Heaven forever as an angel
jokes.punchline: Fry's childlike focus on the fun aerobatics rather than spirituality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry childishness - missing the profound to focus on the playful aspect

jokes.text: Metaphor? Isn't that just a fancy word for 'steaming load'?
jokes.setup: Learning Heaven is a metaphor rather than a real place
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude dismissal of abstract concepts
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender cynicism and crudeness applied to spiritual concepts

jokes.text: I wouldn't call it fancy.
jokes.setup: Bender calling metaphor a 'fancy word for steaming load'
jokes.punchline: Preacherbot's understated correction focusing on the wrong part
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response that misses Bender's actual insult to focus on trivial detail

jokes.text: Maybe we'll meet again in a better metaphor.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently dying and saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: References earlier discussion about metaphors in dying words
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that works as both dying words and reference to earlier theological debate

jokes.text: If you were dead, how could you ask such a dumb question? You're as alive and stupid as ever.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he's alive after being revived
jokes.punchline: Bender's logic combined with characteristic insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - combining logical reasoning with casual cruelty to his best friend

jokes.text: Shut up, baby, I know it.
jokes.setup: Being called a hero for saving Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature arrogant catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase, perfectly timed but not particularly original

jokes.text: Now, that's dead.
jokes.setup: Bender's dramatic death scene
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact medical assessment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan contrast to the emotional moment - clinical assessment of obvious situation

jokes.text: It means life is meaningless. Lesson learned! Let's hit the road.
jokes.setup: Fry asking what Bender's sacrifice means
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully nihilistic conclusion followed by immediate desire to leave
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - casually nihilistic philosophy delivered with inappropriate cheerfulness

jokes.text: For a quarter, I'm a guru. For a nickel, you get a sticker that says you climbed the mountain.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he's the guru of the mountain
jokes.punchline: Commoditized spirituality with budget options
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of commercialized spirituality and tourist trap mentality

jokes.text: Load? Perhaps. Steaming? Perhaps not.
jokes.setup: Fry calling reincarnation a 'big steaming load'
jokes.punchline: Guru's careful parsing of the insult's components
jokes.explanation: Clever callback with philosophical wordplay - guru taking the insult literally

jokes.text: I'm back, baby! And I'm doing loop-de-loops! Woo!
jokes.setup: Guru explaining reincarnation
jokes.punchline: Bender returns as a bird, fulfilling Fry's earlier loop-de-loop dreams
jokes.explanation: Good callback combining Fry's heaven question with Bender's return via reincarnation

jokes.text: Bite my enlightened metal ass, mortality! I'm 40% aerodynamic!
jokes.setup: Bender triumphant over death as a reincarnated bird
jokes.punchline: His catchphrase modified for spiritual context plus percentage gag
jokes.explanation: Good combination of multiple Bender running gags adapted to the situation

jokes.text: Blah?
jokes.setup: Fry praising Leela as 'brave and good and articulate'
jokes.punchline: King's confused/dismissive reaction to the word 'articulate'
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing King's confusion at a basic word

jokes.text: I've never been so angry and hungry.
jokes.setup: King furious about being challenged
jokes.punchline: Conflating anger with hunger in his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing confusion of emotions, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: She needs salt.
jokes.setup: King saying his wife needs no seasoning
jokes.punchline: Fry's practical culinary advice about the portrait/food
jokes.explanation: Simple callback with practical cooking advice in inappropriate context

jokes.text: Well, I stayed behind last time, so...
jokes.setup: Only six can fit in the ship for the Saturn mission
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's hopeful attempt to avoid being excluded again
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg pathos - trying to avoid exclusion but being too pathetic

jokes.text: I suggest Zoidberg. Second!
jokes.setup: Need to exclude someone from the trip
jokes.punchline: Immediate unanimous agreement to exclude Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective Zoidberg abuse - the speed of the 'second' is the timing

jokes.text: Why doesn't anybody like me?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg alone watching TV after being excluded
jokes.punchline: Direct, pathetic plea for understanding his unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Standard Zoidberg self-pity, effective for character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Can you pause it? I'm getting a call.
jokes.setup: News report about mysterious deadly video and phone calls
jokes.punchline: Amy interrupts to answer exactly the dangerous call they're hearing about
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - character doing exactly what they were just warned against

jokes.text: Your car warranty has expired. Press one now, whether or not you want to renew.
jokes.setup: Mysterious deadly phone call
jokes.punchline: It's just a car warranty scam with no real choice
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of horror trope - mysterious deadly call is just annoying spam

jokes.text: We don't want any! What do they think, we're idiots?
jokes.setup: Another warranty scam call while crew member is missing/dead
jokes.punchline: Hermes dismissing it as obvious scam while missing the real danger
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - characters missing obvious pattern while feeling smart

jokes.text: Some ancient horror is picking us off one by one, starting with the most gullible.
jokes.setup: Crew members disappearing after phone calls
jokes.punchline: Professor correctly identifies pattern but insults the victims
jokes.explanation: Good horror genre awareness with characteristic Professor insensitivity

jokes.text: Someone has to stay behind to answer the phones.
jokes.setup: Leaving Zoidberg behind while chasing the phone scam monster
jokes.punchline: Leaving him to do exactly the dangerous thing they're investigating
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup - leaving Zoidberg in the exact dangerous situation they're fleeing

jokes.text: Sweet Subaru of Timbuktu! It's a chop shop.
jokes.setup: Discovering where the car parts are being taken
jokes.punchline: Professor's car-themed exclamation for discovering car crime
jokes.explanation: Creative exclamation that fits both the Professor's style and the automotive context

jokes.text: No. Villains have more charisma. You're just boring.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg revealing himself as the villain
jokes.punchline: Professor's dismissive assessment of Zoidberg's villainy
jokes.explanation: Perfect insult that denies Zoidberg even the dignity of being a proper villain

jokes.text: So, I included all of you, why not? Now, I have Fry's roof rack, Amy's bumper, and, spoiler alert, Leela's spoiler!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining his inclusion scheme
jokes.punchline: 'Spoiler alert' pun for literally revealing Leela's car spoiler
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that works on multiple levels - literal spoiler reveal and car part spoiler

jokes.text: This isn't the rule book, by the way. It's a takeout menu. I'm gonna have the pad Thai.
jokes.setup: Consulting rules for allowing a champion in the duel
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he's been reading a menu and making food choice
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with casual food ordering mid-formal-duel

jokes.text: Fry, you're really bad at fighting. That's true.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to fight as Fry's champion
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately agreeing with the assessment of his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry self-awareness and honesty about his limitations

jokes.text: Roar.
jokes.setup: Dramatic duel about to begin
jokes.punchline: King's understated, bored 'roar' as battle cry
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of what should be intimidating battle cry

jokes.text: The water doesn't have an end, numb duck. Haven't you ever seen a map?
jokes.setup: Fry worried about falling off the end of water
jokes.punchline: Leela's logical explanation calling Fry 'numb duck' (rubber duck)
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate insult that fits the rubber duck context

jokes.text: I made this map, and whole sections were based on old legends and random hunches.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether water has an edge
jokes.punchline: Professor admitting his map is unreliable speculation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor characterization - casual admission of scientific incompetence

jokes.text: With no landmarks, it's hard to say. There's a good chance we never moved at all.
jokes.setup: Asked how far they've traveled at sea
jokes.punchline: Possibility they haven't moved despite long journey
jokes.explanation: Good existential joke about the nature of progress and measurement

jokes.text: Land ho! What's land? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Traditional sailor's call of 'Land ho!'
jokes.punchline: Neither character knows what land actually is
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on exploration - discovering something completely foreign to their experience

jokes.text: Look at his eyeballs! It's the scurvy!
jokes.setup: Duck observing the human visitors
jokes.punchline: Diagnosing normal human eyes as diseased
jokes.explanation: Decent role reversal - ducks seeing humans as diseased/strange

jokes.text: Let's just trade our guns for tobacco and get out of here.
jokes.setup: First contact between rubber ducks and humans
jokes.punchline: Reversing historical colonial trading patterns
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical satire reversing colonial exploitation patterns

jokes.text: We call our land water.
jokes.setup: Ducks explaining they call their home 'land'
jokes.punchline: Humans revealing opposite terminology for same concept
jokes.explanation: Good conceptual reversal about perspective and terminology

jokes.text: Except I can't fall, and I don't have heels.
jokes.setup: Duck saying she's falling head over heels in love
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of the metaphor given her duck anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of metaphor that highlights species differences

jokes.text: Well, in our culture, we believe a woman's sexuality is her own decision! Okay, then.
jokes.setup: Conflict over interspecies romance being forbidden
jokes.punchline: Progressive statement immediately accepted, deflating the conflict
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expected conflict - reasonable position immediately accepted

jokes.text: This means war! War? Well, we don't have that word, but this definitely means peace-fighting!
jokes.setup: Declaring war over the romance conflict
jokes.punchline: Ducks don't have word for war, substitute 'peace-fighting'
jokes.explanation: Excellent oxymoron that highlights peaceful duck nature while maintaining conflict

jokes.text: Rubber in the hole!
jokes.setup: Golf-style battle between ducks and humans
jokes.punchline: Duck version of 'fire in the hole' warning
jokes.explanation: Good species-appropriate adaptation of military terminology

jokes.text: Our love is eternal. It'll live on forever, even after it dies.
jokes.setup: Duck Fry's dying words about eternal love
jokes.punchline: Logical contradiction about eternal things dying
jokes.explanation: Decent philosophical contradiction in dying speech, though not exceptional

jokes.text: Really? A magic spell? No! A science spell!
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Leela was under a magic spell
jokes.punchline: Leela's indignant correction to 'science spell'
jokes.explanation: Good Futurama blend of sci-fi and fantasy tropes with character-appropriate correction
